title: marsdenia heterophylla
intro: Marsdenia heterophylla, also known as the Indian snakeroot or poison nut, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, particularly in India and Sri Lanka. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and anti-diarrheal effects. The health benefits of Marsdenia heterophylla include treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as providing relief from nausea and vomiting. The plant is often prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and it can also be used to make a paste or powder for topical application.
